title: SSLMate secrets should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: SSLMate API key detected - CRITICAL. SSLMate provides automated SSL/TLS certificate management and monitoring services. Exposed API keys grant unauthorized access to: 1) Issue, revoke, and manage SSL/TLS certificates for your domains, 2) Monitor certificate transparency logs and receive alerts, 3) Access certificate lifecycle management functions, 4) View and modify certificate configurations, 5) Access domain validation records and DNS settings, 6) Potentially redirect or intercept HTTPS traffic through certificate manipulation, 7) Abuse certificate issuance limits and quotas, 8) Access billing and subscription information. Certificate management access can lead to severe security incidents including man-in-the-middle attacks, domain hijacking, certificate mis-issuance, and unauthorized certificate revocations that could disrupt services. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1) Revoke the exposed SSLMate API key immediately through the SSLMate dashboard (https://sslmate.com/account/api_credentials), 2) Remove the key from source code, configuration files, and version control history using git-filter-repo or BFG Repo-Cleaner, 3) Generate a new API key with appropriate permissions and scope limitations, 4) Audit all certificates managed through the compromised key for unauthorized changes, revocations, or new issuances, 5) Review certificate transparency logs for unexpected certificate activity, 6) Check domain validation records and DNS settings for unauthorized modifications, 7) Review account access logs for suspicious API activity or unauthorized access patterns, 8) Implement secure secret management: use environment variables, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, or Azure Key Vault, 9) Enable API key rotation policies with expiration dates, 10) Restrict API key permissions to minimum required scopes (principle of least privilege), 11) Implement IP allowlisting for API key usage if possible, 12) Set up monitoring and alerts for certificate management operations, 13) Review and update incident response procedures for certificate compromise scenarios.

SSLMate API key detected in variable assignment - CRITICAL. This SSLMate API key provides full access to your SSL/TLS certificate management infrastructure. Exposure enables attackers to issue fraudulent certificates, revoke legitimate certificates causing service outages, modify certificate configurations, access domain validation systems, and potentially conduct man-in-the-middle attacks. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1) Revoke compromised key at https://sslmate.com/account/api_credentials immediately, 2) Purge from all repositories and version control history, 3) Generate new API key with restricted permissions, 4) Audit all certificate operations for unauthorized activity, 5) Review certificate transparency logs for suspicious issuances, 6) Check domain DNS records for unauthorized modifications, 7) Implement secure credential storage using secret management systems, 8) Enable API key rotation and expiration policies, 9) Set up monitoring for certificate management operations, 10) Restrict API access by IP address if supported.
